creative_brief: Clean, minimal design with a hero image of cattle on pasture. Emphasis on the 18-month timeline with visual contrast against industrial alternatives.
subject: The craft behind your Sunday roast
preheader: What 18 months of patience looks like on your plate
body:
Hi {first_name},

This week we're at the farm.

Yesterday, I watched James from Little Yarran Farm load the last of his 18-month grass-fed cattle onto the truck.

Eighteen months. That's how long it takes to craft beef the way nature intended — no shortcuts, no feedlots, just patient rotational grazing that builds soil while building flavour.

Most supermarket beef? Finished in feedlots after 12 months, then sits in cold storage for weeks before it reaches your plate.

James' beef will be at our hub in 3 days. Days old, not weeks old. You'll taste the difference in every bite — the depth that only comes from cattle raised entirely on pasture, the tenderness that patient farming creates.

This is what farm-direct means. No middlemen marking up prices. No weeks in storage losing flavour. Just the craft of regenerative farming, straight from paddock to pick-up.

We're building something different here — a grocery store that shows you exactly where your food comes from and rewards the farmers doing it right.

Be part of making it happen.
primary_text: Yesterday, I watched James from Little Yarran Farm load the last of his 18-month grass-fed cattle onto the truck.
headline: The craft behind your Sunday roast
